steps.title: Determine the linear equation from the graph
steps.content:
Reading two clear points from the graph: at $x = 0$, $y \approx 65°C$, and at $x = 100$, $y \approx 105°C$.

The slope is $$\text{slope} = \frac{105 - 65}{100 - 0} = \frac{40}{100} = \frac{2}{5}$$ This establishes the linear equation $y = 65 + \frac{2}{5}x$ and confirms that choice A is true.

steps.title: Test choice D at x = 98
steps.content: For a DNA molecule with $x = 98\%$ GC content, use the equation: $$y = 65 + \frac{2}{5}(98) = 65 + 39.2 = 104.2°C$$ The calculated temperature of $104.2°C$ is far above the claimed range of $80°C$ to $90°C$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
